prevalence.value: variable; 1-10 per 100,000/year in high-risk coastal/monsoon areas (often underdiagnosed)
prevalence.trend: stable with monsoon-linked peaks; increased risk with flooding events

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion with lab confirmation (IgM ELISA, MAT, PCR where available); differential includes dengue, malaria, viral hepatitis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; seasonal/outbreak surveillance in high-risk districts
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of thousands/year in high-burden states (including underrecognized cases)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; outcomes depend on early antibiotics and supportive care access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: IgM ELISA commonly used; MAT/PCR in reference labs; clinical diagnosis often in peripheral centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Doxycycline/amoxicillin for mild disease; IV penicillin/ceftriaxone for severe cases; renal support when needed
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis and overlap with dengue/malaria; limited lab access in rural areas during monsoon surges
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: ICMR and state programmes support outbreak detection and clinical management guidance in high-risk regions
